Notice Of Motionand Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jul-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Peter A. Kleinbrodt for defendant and moving party Richard Bouillet
Baron L. Miller for plaintiff and opposing party Lynn M. Nightingale
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
On calendar Wednesday, July 5 2006. 1. Defendant Richard Brouillet's motion to compel responses to Request for Production of Documents is granted, subject to the following conditions and limitations;
2. Plaintiff shall produce documents as requested as to the following matters:
a. Estate of Buquia;
b. Estate of Christiana
c. Estate of Conner
d. Estate of Hardcastle
e. Estate of Logan
f. Conservatorship of Schelgel
g. Estate of Scheif
h. "Office Administration" charges, as listed in plaintiff's Bill of Particulars.

3. With reference to Request For Production No. 7, plaintiff's response may be limited to engagement letters, advertisements, fee schedules, or other similar documents that set forth fees and charges that plaintiff charged for services during the relevant period of the request for production.
4. To the extent that documents requested are public records-i.e., documents filed in court in connection with a probate estate or other legal proceeding, plaintiff need only identify the documents, and specify the action in which the document is filed.
5. To the extent that documents, files or records previously in plaintiff's possession have been turned over to defendant (or a third party), plaintiff's verified response shall identify the documents and specify that they have been transferred to defendant or the named third party.
6. Request for sanctions is denied.
Plaintiff shall serve her verified responses to the subject Request For Production of Documents within 10 days. Commissioner: PAUL SLAVIT
